description: S3 protocol compatibility — close gaps in ACL/policy/IAM/checksums/SSE/notifications/website/replication/auth so Strata passes >=80% of Ceph s3-tests executable subset.

userStories.title: Parse AccessControlPolicy XML body for PutBucketAcl/PutObjectAcl
userStories.description: As a client, I want PutBucketAcl/PutObjectAcl to accept a full XML grant list, not just the canned x-amz-acl header, so I can grant fine-grained permissions to specific principals.
acceptanceCriteria: Parse <AccessControlPolicy> with <Owner> and <AccessControlList><Grant> entries
Persist grant list in meta.Store via new SetBucketGrants/GetBucketGrants and SetObjectGrants/GetObjectGrants methods (memory + cassandra)
GetBucketAcl/GetObjectAcl return the persisted grants when present, fall back to canned otherwise
Reject grants with unknown grantee Type or Permission values with 400 MalformedACLError
Unit tests cover: round-trip XML body, mixed canned header + body, malformed body 400, unknown grantee type 400
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... ./internal/meta/... passes
userStories.notes: Grants stored as JSON blob (bucket_acl_grants table for buckets, grants column on objects). Memory store uses bucketGrants/objectGrants maps.

userStories.title: Add IAM-style policy evaluator package internal/auth/policy
userStories.description: As a follow-on for bucket policy enforcement, I need a standalone evaluator that takes a policy document, principal, action, resource, and context, and returns Allow/Deny.
acceptanceCriteria: New package internal/auth/policy with Document, Statement, Evaluate(doc, principal, action, resource, ctx) (Decision, error)
Supports Effect (Allow/Deny), Principal (string, list, or *), Action (string or list, with s3:* wildcards), Resource (string or list, with arn wildcards), Condition (StringEquals only)
Explicit Deny overrides Allow
Default decision when no statement matches: Deny
Unit tests cover: explicit allow, explicit deny override, wildcard action, wildcard resource, StringEquals condition match/miss, anonymous principal *
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/auth/... passes
userStories.notes: Package internal/auth/policy: Decision (Allow/Deny), Document, Statement, Principal (string '*' or {AWS:[]}), StringOrSlice for Action/Resource, glob matcher for s3:* and ARN wildcards, StringEquals condition only, explicit Deny overrides. Default Deny on no match or nil doc. Parse() validates Effect.

userStories.title: Wire policy evaluator into request middleware and object handlers
userStories.description: As a bucket owner, I want my JSON bucket policy to actually gate object-level requests; today it is stored but never enforced.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/auth middleware loads the bucket's policy document via meta.Store after SigV4/anonymous identity is resolved
Object handlers (PutObject, GetObject, HeadObject, DeleteObject, multipart endpoints) call requireAccess(action, arn) before any state change
Anonymous identity passes through evaluator with principal '*'
Policy denial returns 403 AccessDenied
Unit tests: anonymous GET allowed by s3:GetObject Allow, anonymous GET denied without, explicit Deny overrides Allow, wrong-principal Allow denied
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes
userStories.notes: internal/s3api/access.go added requireAccess(b, action, arn). Loads bucket policy via Meta.GetBucketPolicy (ErrNoSuchBucketPolicy → bypass), parses with policy.Parse, evaluates with principalForRequest (anonymous → '*'). 403 AccessDenied on Deny/no-match. Wired into handleObject for PUT/GET/HEAD/DELETE + initiateMultipart, uploadPart, completeMultipart, abortMultipart, listParts, copyObject. ?acl/?tagging/?retention/?legal-hold subresources are out of scope.

userStories.title: Enforce bucket and object ACL grants on object-level requests
userStories.description: As a bucket owner, when bucket ACL is public-read, anonymous GETs should succeed; when private, they should 403. Today the canned ACL is stored but never consulted on the request path.
acceptanceCriteria: Anonymous GET /<bucket>/<key> succeeds when bucket ACL is public-read or public-read-write
Anonymous PUT succeeds only when ACL is public-read-write
Per-object grants from US-001 override bucket ACL on per-object reads
ACL check runs after policy check; explicit policy Deny still wins
Unit tests cover four canned values × {GET, PUT, DELETE} × {anonymous, owner, alt principal}
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes
userStories.notes: internal/s3api/access.go grew requireACL + cannedAllows + grantsAllow + granteeMatches helpers; requireObjectAccess wraps requireAccess (policy) then requireACL (canned + persisted grants). Owner FC: info.Owner==b.Owner short-circuits (anonymous-owned bucket + anonymous request still passes since both Owner=='anonymous'). Per-object grants override bucket ACL on s3:GetObject only. testutil_test.go gained X-Test-Principal injection so tests can simulate owner/alt principals.

userStories.title: Add Cassandra-backed credentials store
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to add and rotate access keys without restarting the gateway, so I can onboard new clients in production.
acceptanceCriteria: New cassandra.CredentialsStore implementing the auth.Store interface against the access_keys table
Schema migration adds access_keys (access_key text PK, secret_key text, owner text, disabled boolean, created_at timestamp) via additive ALTER
Composable auth.MultiStore tries static credentials first, then Cassandra
60s in-memory cache with refresh-on-miss and explicit Invalidate(accessKey)
Unit tests cover lookup hit/miss/disabled, key rotation, deleted-key returns auth.ErrCredentialNotFound
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/auth/... passes
userStories.notes: auth.CredentialsStore is the existing iface (not auth.Store); auth.ErrNoSuchCredential is the existing sentinel (not auth.ErrCredentialNotFound) — used both verbatim. New cassandra.CredentialStore (internal/meta/cassandra/credentials.go) selects from access_keys (PK=access_key); disabled rows are surfaced as ErrNoSuchCredential. Schema: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS access_keys appended to tableDDL (additive). New auth.MultiStore (internal/auth/multi.go) tries stores in order, caches positive AND negative results for ttl, exposes Invalidate(accessKey); transient (non-ErrNoSuchCredential) errors propagate without caching. Wired in cmd/strata-gateway/main.go: static store first; cassandra.CredentialStore appended only when meta backend is cassandra (via metaStore.(*metacassandra.Store).Session()). DefaultCacheTTL = 60s exported for callers.

userStories.title: Add IAM users + access keys storage and CreateUser/ListUsers/DeleteUser endpoints
userStories.description: As an admin tool, I want to call POST /?Action=CreateUser etc. against the gateway to provision principals so s3-tests fixtures ([iam root], [iam alt root]) can spin up isolated identities.
acceptanceCriteria: iam_users table in cassandra (user_name PK, user_id, path, created_at) plus mirror in memory store
internal/s3api/iam.go routes ?Action=CreateUser, ?Action=GetUser, ?Action=ListUsers, ?Action=DeleteUser
AWS-style XML form-encoded request/response shapes match the iam:CreateUser docs (CreateUserResponse + CreateUserResult > User)
Hardcoded [iam root] credentials gate all ?Action= endpoints
Unit tests cover round-trip CreateUser → GetUser → ListUsers → DeleteUser, double-create returns 409 EntityAlreadyExists, delete-missing returns 404 NoSuchEntity
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: Add CreateAccessKey/ListAccessKeys/DeleteAccessKey IAM endpoints
userStories.description: As an admin tool, I want CreateAccessKey to mint a (key, secret) pair tied to a user so s3-tests fixtures can authenticate.
acceptanceCriteria: ?Action=CreateAccessKey, ?Action=ListAccessKeys, ?Action=DeleteAccessKey routed in internal/s3api/iam.go
CreateAccessKey writes to access_keys (US-005 schema) with user_name foreign key and returns AccessKey + SecretAccessKey in XML once
DeleteAccessKey removes the row and invalidates the cache (US-005 Invalidate)
Hardcoded [iam root] gate enforced
Unit tests cover CreateAccessKey → use → DeleteAccessKey → 403 InvalidAccessKeyId on next request
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: Implement minimal STS AssumeRole endpoint
userStories.description: As a multi-account test fixture, I want temporary credentials with an expiry so cross-account scenarios in s3-tests can run.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /?Action=AssumeRole returns AccessKeyId, SecretAccessKey, SessionToken, Expiration (1h default)
SigV4 middleware honors x-amz-security-token header against the temporary key store
Token storage in-memory with TTL eviction (acceptable given short expiry)
Expired token → 403 ExpiredToken
Unit tests cover assume → use → expire → 403
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/auth/... ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: Verify and persist x-amz-checksum-* headers on PutObject (CRC32, CRC32C, SHA1, SHA256, CRC64NVME)
userStories.description: As an AWS SDK v3 client (which sends checksums by default), I want my PutObject requests with x-amz-checksum-<algo> to succeed and have the checksum stored.
acceptanceCriteria: Add meta.Object.Checksums map[string]string with additive Cassandra schema migration
Parse x-amz-checksum-<algo> request headers; verify against streamed body content during PutChunks
Mismatch → 400 BadDigest
Return x-amz-checksum-<algo> on GetObject and HeadObject
Five algorithms supported: crc32, crc32c, sha1, sha256, crc64nvme (vendor a small CRC64NVME implementation if needed; AWS test vector covered)
Unit tests per algorithm: positive, mismatch, missing optional
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: Multipart UploadPart and CompleteMultipartUpload checksum support
userStories.description: As an SDK v3 client uploading multipart, I want each part's checksum verified and the composite checksum returned by CompleteMultipartUpload.
acceptanceCriteria: UploadPart accepts and verifies x-amz-checksum-<algo> per part
Per-part checksum stored in meta.MultipartPart.Checksums
CompleteMultipartUpload computes composite checksum (algo-specific concat-and-hash rule) and returns x-amz-checksum-<algo> header
Mismatch on any part → 400 BadDigest from UploadPart, never reaches Complete
Unit tests round-trip a 3-part multipart with crc32 and verify response
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: SSE-S3 metadata storage (passthrough) on objects and bucket default
userStories.description: As a client setting x-amz-server-side-encryption: AES256, I want the header accepted, persisted, and echoed back on Get/Head — even though Strata does not actually encrypt at rest yet.
acceptanceCriteria: meta.Object.SSE field stores algorithm string (additive Cassandra ALTER)
PutObject + InitiateMultipartUpload accept x-amz-server-side-encryption (AES256 only for now; reject aws:kms with NotImplemented)
GetObject/HeadObject return the same header
PutBucketEncryption/GetBucketEncryption/DeleteBucketEncryption endpoints round-trip the XML config blob via the existing setBucketBlob helpers
When bucket encryption default is set and a PutObject omits the header, the default is applied
Unit tests: round-trip per object, round-trip per bucket default, default applied on PUT
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: SSE-C customer-key headers — accept, validate, gate reads
userStories.description: As a client uploading with SSE-C, I want the gateway to accept and validate the customer key headers and reject Get requests that omit them.
acceptanceCriteria: PutObject parses x-amz-server-side-encryption-customer-{algorithm,key,key-MD5}, validates base64 key length 32 bytes and MD5 match
Persist key MD5 only in meta.Object.SSECKeyMD5 (additive ALTER)
GetObject without matching headers when SSECKeyMD5 set → 400 InvalidRequest
GetObject with mismatched MD5 → 400 AccessDenied
CopyObject parses x-amz-copy-source-server-side-encryption-customer-* (mirror)
Unit tests: positive PUT+GET round-trip, missing key on GET, wrong MD5 on PUT, wrong MD5 on GET
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: GetObjectAttributes endpoint
userStories.description: As a client preparing a multipart copy, I want a single low-cost call returning ETag, Checksum, ObjectParts, ObjectSize, StorageClass instead of HEAD + ListParts.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /<bucket>/<key>?attributes route in internal/s3api/server.go
Honors x-amz-object-attributes header (comma-separated subset; only listed fields are populated)
XML response per AWS GetObjectAttributesOutput schema
Unit tests: full response, partial via header, missing key 404, missing object-attributes header 400
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: RestoreObject stub endpoint with x-amz-restore status
userStories.description: As a client calling POST ?restore, I want a 200 acknowledgment so SDKs that issue restore-on-read for GLACIER objects do not fail outright. Restore is a no-op since Strata has no archival tier yet.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /<bucket>/<key>?restore parses RestoreRequest XML
Returns 200 OK if object exists, 404 if not
meta.Object.RestoreStatus field set; surfaced on HEAD as x-amz-restore: ongoing-request="false", expiry-date="..."
Malformed XML → 400 MalformedXML
Unit tests: round-trip, 404, malformed XML
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: PutBucketObjectLockConfiguration / GetBucketObjectLockConfiguration with default-retention inheritance
userStories.description: As a regulated client, I want bucket-level default retention so new objects inherit it. Today only object-level retention works.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT /<bucket>?object-lock parses ObjectLockConfiguration XML and persists per bucket via setBucketBlob helper
GET /<bucket>?object-lock returns the persisted XML or 404 ObjectLockConfigurationNotFoundError
PutObject inherits Mode and RetainUntilDate from bucket default when request headers omit them
CreateBucket with x-amz-bucket-object-lock-enabled: true sets the bucket flag; this flag is required for object-lock config
Unit tests: round-trip config, inheritance on PutObject, locked PUT cannot be deleted under COMPLIANCE
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: PutBucketNotificationConfiguration / GetBucketNotificationConfiguration (storage-only)
userStories.description: As a client configuring event notifications, I want the gateway to accept and return my notification configuration XML, even if Strata has no event publisher yet.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT/GET /<bucket>?notification round-trips the XML blob via setBucketBlob/getBucketBlob
Schema validation: at least one of TopicConfiguration, QueueConfiguration, LambdaFunctionConfiguration
Empty body on PUT clears the config (returns NoSuchConfiguration on subsequent GET)
Unit tests: round-trip, malformed body 400, empty body clears
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: PutBucketWebsite / GetBucketWebsite / DeleteBucketWebsite + index document routing
userStories.description: As a static-site host, I want bucket website config and have GET on the bucket serve the configured index document when present.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT/GET/DELETE /<bucket>?website round-trips the XML blob
When website config is present, GET /<bucket>/ serves the configured IndexDocument object
When path matches no key, serve the configured ErrorDocument with 404
Without website config, current behaviour (ListObjects) is preserved
Unit tests: round-trip XML; index serving; error doc; without config
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: PutBucketReplication / GetBucketReplication / DeleteBucketReplication (storage-only) + status header
userStories.description: As a client wiring up cross-region replication on the source side, I want the configuration round-trip and PutObject to set replication status header when a rule's filter matches.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT/GET/DELETE /<bucket>?replication round-trips XML via setBucketBlob
Validation: at least one Rule with Destination
PutObject sets x-amz-replication-status: PENDING only when at least one rule's Filter (prefix + tags) matches the object key
Buckets with replication configured but no matching rule do not emit the header
Unit tests: round-trip, status header presence on match, status header absence on no-match
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: PutBucketLogging / GetBucketLogging
userStories.description: As an audit setup, I want to round-trip server-access logging config on a bucket.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT/GET /<bucket>?logging round-trips XML
Empty body on PUT clears the config
Subsequent GET on cleared bucket returns valid empty BucketLoggingStatus document
Unit tests: round-trip, clear, empty-on-fresh-bucket
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: PutBucketTagging / GetBucketTagging / DeleteBucketTagging
userStories.description: As a billing/audit consumer, I want bucket-level tags. Object tagging already works.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT/GET/DELETE /<bucket>?tagging round-trips per AWS Tagging schema (Tag.Key/Tag.Value)
Empty body on PUT or absent config returns 404 NoSuchTagSet on GET
Limit: 50 tags per bucket (matches AWS); over-limit → 400 InvalidTag
Unit tests: round-trip, 404, over-limit
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: GetBucketLocation + x-amz-bucket-region on HEAD bucket + LocationConstraint body parsing on CreateBucket
userStories.description: As an SDK doing pre-flight region detection, I want GET /<bucket>?location and HEAD /<bucket> to report the configured region; CreateBucket with a LocationConstraint XML body should persist the region.
acceptanceCriteria: Add meta.Bucket.Region field via additive Cassandra ALTER
CreateBucket parses optional <CreateBucketConfiguration><LocationConstraint> body and stores Region
GET /<bucket>?location returns <LocationConstraint> with the region (empty for default)
HEAD /<bucket> sets x-amz-bucket-region header from the stored region (or 'default')
Unit tests: CreateBucket with region body → GET location returns it; HEAD has the header; no body still works
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: Per-chunk signature validation in STREAMING-AWS4-HMAC-SHA256-PAYLOAD
userStories.description: As a security-conscious operator, I want the chained per-chunk HMAC verified — today we decode the framing but skip the chain so an attacker can mutate chunked uploads.
acceptanceCriteria:
Implement chain in internal/auth: sig(chunk_n) = HMAC(signing_key, "AWS4-HMAC-SHA256-PAYLOAD
<date>
<scope>
<prev-sig>
<hash("")>
<hash(chunk)>")
Reject mismatched chunk → 403 SignatureDoesNotMatch (drops the body and the connection)
Final empty chunk also verified
No STRATA_AUTH_RELAXED_STREAMING flag — strict by default
Unit tests: positive AWS doc test vector; mutated chunk rejected; missing final chunk rejected
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/auth/... passes

userStories.title: CompleteMultipartUpload idempotency on retry
userStories.description: As a flaky-network client, I want a retry of CompleteMultipartUpload after a successful first attempt to return success, not LWT failure.
acceptanceCriteria: After successful Complete, persist (uploadID, etag, version_id, completed_at) for 10 minutes via meta.Store
Second Complete with same uploadID returns the stored CompleteMultipartUploadResult XML and the same ETag
After 10 minutes, returns NoSuchUpload
TTL applies in cassandra (USING TTL) and via timestamp in memory store
Unit tests: success → retry returns same XML; expiry → 404
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... ./internal/meta/... passes

userStories.title: Anonymous identity + STRATA_AUTH_MODE flag
userStories.description: As an unauthenticated client requesting a public bucket, I want the request to skip SigV4 and be authorized by the bucket policy/ACL when STRATA_AUTH_MODE=optional.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/auth.Identity gains IsAnonymous bool
config.Config.AuthMode supports values 'required' (default), 'optional', 'disabled'
When mode=optional and request has no Authorization header and no presigned query, middleware sets auth.AnonymousIdentity and proceeds
Anonymous identity flows into policy/ACL evaluation (US-003, US-004)
Mode=required preserves current 403 behaviour
Unit tests: anon on public bucket OK in optional mode; anon on private bucket 403; anon in required mode always 403
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/auth/... ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: MFA Delete header gating
userStories.description: As a bucket owner with MFA Delete enabled, I want delete-version requests without an x-amz-mfa header to be rejected.
acceptanceCriteria: PutBucketVersioning accepts <MfaDelete>Enabled</MfaDelete> in the body and persists
DeleteObjectVersion (delete with versionId) requires x-amz-mfa: <serial> <code> when MfaDelete is enabled
Static-config TOTP secret accepted via STRATA_MFA_SECRETS=<serial>:<base32-secret> env var
Bucket without MfaDelete enabled accepts DELETE without the header (current behaviour)
Unit tests: enabled + missing header → 403; enabled + valid TOTP → 204; disabled → 204 always; invalid TOTP → 403
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: CopyObject metadata + tagging directives + copy-source preconditions
userStories.description: As a client copying with metadata changes, I want x-amz-metadata-directive REPLACE to honour new content-type and user-meta headers, COPY to preserve source metadata, and copy-source-if-* preconditions to short-circuit.
acceptanceCriteria: Parse x-amz-metadata-directive (default COPY); on REPLACE use request headers, on COPY copy from source object
Parse x-amz-tagging-directive (default COPY); same semantics for tags
Honour x-amz-copy-source-if-match, if-none-match, if-modified-since, if-unmodified-since; mismatch → 412 PreconditionFailed
Unit tests cover each directive value × precondition match/miss matrix
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: ListBuckets returns authenticated owner + filters by ownership
userStories.description: Today the response hardcodes <Owner>strata</Owner> and returns every bucket. With multi-tenant credentials, every owner sees everyone's identity. Filter and label by auth.FromContext(r.Context()).Owner.
acceptanceCriteria: Response <Owner> reflects the authenticated principal (ID and DisplayName)
ListBuckets only returns buckets where meta.Bucket.Owner equals the authenticated principal
Anonymous (when allowed) sees an empty bucket list
Unit tests: two principals see disjoint bucket lists; anonymous gets empty list
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: Run s3-tests after each block; record pass-rate baseline
userStories.description: As a maintainer, after each block ships I want the s3-tests pass rate re-measured and committed so we have a tracked compatibility number.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/s3-tests/run.sh executes successfully against the running gateway with the default filter
scripts/s3-tests/README.md baseline section updated with the new pass-rate line including date and commit short SHA
Pass rate on executable subset is at least 80% (>=16/19 with growth as new endpoints unblock previously filtered tests)
If pass rate drops below the previous run, the regression is enumerated in the README baseline section
go build ./... passes
go test ./... passes
